description_long: This object is a historical-style rifle with a short, wide barrel and a robust, dark metallic body. The barrel is cylindrical and flares slightly at the muzzle, suggesting a design reminiscent of a blunderbuss or early musket. The stock is made of dark wood, ergonomically shaped with finger grooves for a secure grip, and features a pronounced pistol grip style. The trigger and hammer mechanism are exposed, with visible metallic components and a classic, old-fashioned firing mechanism. The overall finish is a combination of polished metal and stained wood, giving it a vintage, well-crafted appearance. The rifle lacks modern attachments, focusing on a simple, functional design typical of early firearms.
description: A vintage-style rifle with a flared metal barrel and a dark wooden pistol grip stock, featuring exposed trigger and hammer mechanisms.
description_view_0: The rifle is viewed head-on from the muzzle, showing the wide, flared barrel opening and a narrow silhouette.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the full length of the barrel, the wooden pistol grip stock, and the exposed trigger and hammer.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the wooden grip and the alignment of the barrel, with the hammer and trigger mechanism partially visible.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the same features as the left, with clear visibility of the barrel, grip, and firing mechanism.
